records.ety: [OE. flai, flaw flake; cf. Sw. flaga flaw, crack, breach, flake, D. vlaag gust of wind, Norw. flage, flaag, and E. flag a flat stone.]

defs: A crack or breach; a gap or fissure; a defect of continuity or cohesion; as, a flaw in a knife or a vase.
quotes: This heart Shall break into a hundered thousand flaws.

defs: A defect; a fault; as, a flaw in reputation; a flaw in a will, in a deed, or in a statute.
quotes: Has not this also its flaws and its dark side?

defs: A sudden burst of noise and disorder; a tumult; uproar; a quarrel.
quotes: And deluges of armies from the town Came pouring in; I heard the mighty flaw.

defs: A sudden burst or gust of wind of short duration.
quotes: Snow, and hail, and stormy gust and flaw.
Like flaws in summer laying lusty corn.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Flawed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Flawing.]

defs: To crack; to make flaws in.
quotes: The brazen caldrons with the frosts are flawed.

defs: To break; to violate; to make of no effect.
